header.title: Documentation
header.subtitle: Guides, references, and system architecture for Workforce.

welcome.title: Welcome to Workforce
welcome.body: Workforce is an event-based hiring management system built for large-scale operations in Saudi Arabia. It covers the entire hiring lifecycle — from posting jobs and screening candidates to scheduling interviews, managing workforce placements, and tracking event campaign progress.

dashboard.label: Dashboard
dashboard.desc: Overview of active events, open positions, scheduled interviews, and recent applications. Quick-action buttons for common tasks.

events.label: Events & SMP
events.desc: Create and manage hiring events (e.g. Hajj 2026, Ramadan). Each event tracks its own timeline, headcount, and status.

jobs.label: Job Applications
jobs.desc: Post jobs linked to events or standalone. Filter by status (Active, Draft, Ended) and manage postings.

interviews.label: Interview Calls
interviews.desc: Track scheduled interviews, their type (Video, Phone, In-Person), interviewer assignments, and current status.

workforce.label: Workforce
workforce.desc: View active workforce placements. See who is hired, their event assignment, and shift coverage.

talent.label: Talent
talent.desc: Full candidate database with search, filters, and pagination. Supports 70,000+ candidates with fast server-side queries.

automation.label: Rules & Automation
automation.desc: Configure automated triggers — e.g. send SMS when a candidate applies, or flag incomplete profiles.

notifications.label: Notification Center
notifications.desc: Manage SMS integrations (GoInfinito). Configure credentials and test connections.

settings.label: System & Settings
settings.desc: System-level configuration, user management, and platform preferences.

postJob.title: How to Post a Job
postJob.desc: Create a job application — standalone or linked to a hiring event.

1.title: Go to Job Applications
1.desc: Click 'Job Applications' in the left sidebar.

2.title: Fill in job details
2.desc: Click 'Post Job' to create a new position — standalone or linked to a hiring event.

3.title: Fill in the form
3.desc: Enter the job title, type (Full Time / Part Time), location, region, salary range, deadline, description, and requirements.

4.title: Set status
4.desc: Choose 'Draft' to save without publishing, or 'Active' to publish immediately and accept applications.

5.title: Submit
5.desc: Click 'Save Draft' or 'Publish Now'. The posting appears in the table immediately.

createEvent.title: How to Create an Event
createEvent.desc: Events group related job postings under one campaign.

2.title: Click 'Create Event'
2.desc: Press the green 'Create Event' button in the top right.

3.title: Name and describe the event
3.desc: Enter a clear name (e.g. 'Hajj 1446') and a short description of scope.

4.title: Set the timeline
4.desc: Choose start and end dates. These define the active window for this event's hiring.

5.title: Submit
5.desc: Click 'Create Event'. It will appear as 'Upcoming' and can be activated when ready.

1.tail: to find by name, national ID, phone number, or city.

2.tail: to narrow by: New, Shortlisted, Interviewed, Hired, Not Shortlisted.

3.tail: to separate Saudi and Non-Saudi candidates.

4.tail: to sort by name, rating, or date added.

5.tail: Pagination shows 50 candidates per page for performance. Use filters to narrow large result sets.

tech.warning: This section is restricted to super_admin and admin roles only. Do not share access credentials or architecture details externally.

frontend.detail: Tailwind CSS v4, Shadcn/UI, TanStack Query v5, wouter routing

backend.detail: REST API, bcryptjs auth, Zod validation

database.detail: drizzle-zod for schema validation, drizzle-kit for migrations

users.notes: Roles: super_admin, admin, hr_manager, recruiter, interviewer, viewer, candidate

candidates.notes: Indexes on status+city, phone, national_id, name, rating. Bulk insert via 1000-row batches.

events.notes: Status enum: upcoming → active → closed → archived

job_postings.notes: Linked to events via event_id FK. Status: draft → active → filled → closed

applications.notes: Unique constraint on (candidate_id, job_id). Status pipeline.

interviews.notes: Linked to candidate + job_posting. Type: video, phone, in_person

workforce.notes: Linked to candidate + event. Tracks check-in/check-out.

automation_rules.notes: Stored in DB, toggled on/off. Executed server-side.

me.desc: Returns current user (dev bypass: always admin)

login.desc: Authenticate by nationalId, phone, email, or username + password

candidatesList.desc: Paginated candidate list. Query: page, limit, search, status, city, nationality, sortBy, sortOrder

candidatesBulk.desc: Bulk insert up to 1,000 candidates per upload

automationUpdate.desc: Toggle or update an automation rule

rbac.title: RBAC Implementation
rbac.outdated: This section is outdated and pending a full rewrite. The RBAC system was rebuilt in T1–T10: roles + permissions + role_permissions tables replaced the legacy user_role enum.
rbac.p1: Roles are now stored in the roles table and edited live in Settings → Roles & Access. Super Admin and Candidate are the only system (immutable) roles; every other role is owner-defined.
rbac.p2: Permission keys are catalogued in shared/permissions.ts (~129 keys grouped by category). Each /api/* route declares its required permission via requirePermission("…") middleware; a boot-time linter fails startup if any route is unguarded.
rbac.p3: The /api/me endpoint returns { role, roleId, isSuperAdmin, permissions[] }. The frontend should gate UI elements by permission key, not role string.

automation.title: Automation Engine
automation.desc: How rules are stored and triggered.
automation.p1: Rules are stored in the automation_rules table with a trigger and action column (text/JSONB).
automation.p2: Each rule has an isActive boolean. Toggling from the UI sends a PATCH /api/automation/:id.
automation.p3: Rule execution is currently client-triggered (demo). Production implementation should add a server-side event bus or job queue (e.g. BullMQ) that listens to application lifecycle events and fires matching rules.

items.1: Re-enable authentication routes in App.tsx (currently bypassed for development)
items.2: Add server-side RBAC middleware to all mutating API endpoints
items.3: Set SESSION_SECRET, DATABASE_URL as environment secrets
items.4: Configure GoInfinito credentials in Notification Center
items.5: Run full unit, system, regression, UAT, and security tests
items.6: Implement bilingual (EN/AR) toggle input across job posting forms
items.7: Set up BullMQ or equivalent for production automation rule execution
items.8: Enable rate limiting on auth and bulk-upload endpoints
items.9: Configure HTTPS / TLS termination in production
